location.office_time_required: four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices

secondary_archetype: AI Enablement / Change Management Program Manager

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in program or project management
Expected to own programs end-to-end without waiting for structure

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in program or project management, preferably in a fast-paced tech environment.
must_haves.qualifier: preferably in a fast-paced tech environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience taking ambiguous problems and building a program around them; narrative, roadmap, milestones, and operating cadence without waiting for structure to be provided
History of driving behavior change, not just program completion, and the judgment to know the difference between a launch and a result
Hands-on experience building or automating workflows using AI tools — able to walk through something personally built, what broke, and what changed as a result.
Experience owning large, distributed programs without direct authority over participants, and keeping them activated and accountable
Communication habits that keep stakeholders informed without them having to ask — status on shared surfaces, not surfaced reactively
Experience defining program health models upfront: success metrics, milestones, and measurement built in before launch, not retroactively
Comfort spending time in a problem before designing a solution
Familiarity with common productivity or workflow tools (e.g., Jira, Asana, Notion, etc)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience running ambassador, champion, or CoE programs; groups where you had no direct authority over participants but were accountable for their activation and outcomes
Background in enablement, L&D, or change management in a technology environment
Experience in an internal AI or enterprise AI adoption role
Hands-on experience with AI tooling (e.g., ChatGPT, Glean, Claude, Cursor, Gemini)
Background in product operations, business operations, or technical program management.
Working knowledge of AI governance or Responsible AI principles.

scope.surface: Applied AI team — AI enablement programs across all of Chime (AI Champions network, onboarding/activation programs, self-serve playbooks, golden paths)
primary_metrics_inferred: Fluency progression of Chimers with AI tools
Adoption signals of AAI-built tools and resources
core_responsibilities: Own cross-functional AI enablement programs end-to-end (e.g., AI Champions network, onboarding and activation programs)
Go deep on workflow problems before proposing solutions
Partner directly with teams to build AI-enabled solutions hands-on
Set health model for programs from day one — success metrics, milestones, and status on shared surfaces
Drive adoption of AAI-built tools and resources
Build and maintain self-serve enablement resources (playbooks, golden paths)
Lead change management for AI enablement work — training, comms, and activation
Build documentation and governance into programs from the start
Measure whether Chimers are actually working differently and report to leadership
Foster collaboration between Product, Engineering, BizOps, and People teams

green_flags: Clear program ownership scope — not a coordination role, genuine autonomy implied
Role is on an Applied AI team with clear internal mandate and executive visibility
Hybrid policy clearly described (4 days in office, Fridays remote for those near an office; fully remote program also mentioned)
Generous benefits including 24 weeks parental leave, $15k family planning reimbursement, wellness stipend
Bonus and equity explicitly mentioned alongside base
red_flags: JD hashtag says #LI-Hybrid but also mentions a 'fully remote program,' creating ambiguity about whether fully remote is truly available for this role
Specific cities for office locations not named in JD body beyond inferring San Francisco from legal disclosures
'Preferably in a fast-paced tech environment' qualifier on years of experience is vague and may be applied inconsistently

ambiguities: JD hashtag #LI-Hybrid suggests hybrid, and the benefits section describes a 4-day-in-office policy, but also references a 'fully remote program' — unclear if this role is eligible for fully remote or hybrid only.

Office cities are not explicitly named for this role; San Francisco inferred from legal disclosures referencing the SF Fair Chance Ordinance and Cook County/NYC/LA ordinances, suggesting multiple office locations exist but the specific role location is not pinned.
